seoTitle: How to Bulk Clone Jira Issues Without Hassle
metaDescription: Learn methods to bulk clone Jira issues, from automation rules to marketplace tools, while preserving subtasks, attachments, and links.
intro: Duplicating tickets one at a time is painful, especially when you need to preserve hierarchies and links. This guide covers two practical approaches to bulk cloning issues in Jira — building an automation rule, or using a marketplace app for larger jobs.

steps.heading: Filter the issues you want to clone
steps.body: Use the advanced issue navigator and a JQL query to isolate the exact group of tasks, bugs, or stories you need to duplicate.

steps.heading: Build a clone automation rule
steps.body: Go to Project Settings, open the Automation builder, and configure a manual trigger that clones matching issues.

steps.heading: Or use a marketplace cloning app
steps.body: For larger jobs, apps like Deep Clone or Easy Clone in your navigator toolbar can process large batches of issues at once.

steps.heading: Choose what to carry over
steps.body: Select your destination project and decide whether to include subtasks, attachments, comments, or links in the cloned copies.

steps.heading: Clean up the cloned issues
steps.body: Use bulk field editing to strip default 'CLONE' prefixes and adjust summaries or assignees before confirming the duplication job.

faq.question: How do I clone multiple Jira issues at once?
faq.answer: You can build an automation rule that clones issues on a trigger, or use a marketplace app like Deep Clone or Easy Clone for larger batches.

faq.question: Does cloning preserve subtasks and attachments?
faq.answer: Most cloning tools let you choose whether to carry over subtasks, attachments, and comment logs when duplicating issues.

faq.question: Why do cloned issues have 'CLONE' in the title?
faq.answer: Jira adds a default prefix to cloned issue summaries, which you can remove afterward using bulk field editing.

faq.question: Can I clone issues across different projects?
faq.answer: Yes, when cloning you can select a different destination project to move the duplicated issues into.
